cases.title: Crouse et al. v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint out of order and moot because the Session had dismissed all underlying charges against three Ruling Elders before the complaint was resolved, and those REs had voluntarily resigned from the Session. The SJC approved the dismissal unanimously 23-0.

cases.title: TE Ryan Biese et al. v. Tennessee Valley Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The complainants withdrew this complaint on July 18, 2022, and the SJC noted the withdrawal on October 20, 2022.

cases.title: Harrell et al. v. Covenant Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained the appeal of seven ruling elders of a PCA mission church who had been convicted and censured with indefinite suspension from the Lord's Supper for opposing the organizing minister's continuation as pastor at particularization. The SJC reversed the guilty verdicts, finding that the indictments were fatally deficient in specificity, the evidence did not support transgressions of the fifth or ninth commandments, and the Session lacked authority to determine who should be elected as pastor of the congregation.

cases.title: RE David Snoke v. Pittsburgh Presbytery
precedent_refs: Case 2021-07 RE Acree v. Tennessee Valley (March 2022, Report to 49th GA, p. 2144)
Case 2020-13 Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 817)
Case 2020-01 Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 801)
Case 2012-08 RE Warren Jackson v. NW Georgia (M43GA, 2015, p. 568)
Case 2012-06 Deacon Don Bethel v. SE Alabama (M41GA, 2013, p. 614)
Case 92-9b Overman v. Eastern Carolina (M21GA, 1993, p. 223)
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint administratively out of order and unable to be put in order because RE Snoke lacked standing to file a complaint against presbytery action, as he was not a commissioner from his church to the presbytery meeting where the challenged action occurred. The SJC voted 20-1 to dismiss (Sartorius dissenting).

cases.title: Benyola v. Central Florida Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint administratively out of order because Mr. Benyola was no longer a member of any PCA congregation and thus lacked standing. The SJC approved the dismissal unanimously 23-0.

cases.title: Presbyterian Church in America v. TE Daniel Herron
precedent_refs: Case 2021-06 Herron et al. v. Central Indiana
Case 2021-14 Herron et al. v. CIP
Case 2021-15 Barber et al. v. CIP
Case 2022-02 Herron et al. v. CIP
cases.synopsis: The SJC conducted a full 42-hour trial of TE Daniel Herron of Central Indiana Presbytery on charges arising from allegations of sexual harassment and bullying, referred to the SJC by CIP under BCO 41. The SJC acquitted TE Herron on all charges, removed all censures and administrative suspensions imposed by CIP, and restored him to good standing as a PCA minister.
